title: Leandra riograndensis
taxonomy: Leandra riograndensis is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to Brazil and belongs to the genus Leandra. The plant is classified as a member of the order Ericales. Leandra riograndensis is further classified as a member of the family Ericaceae and the subfamily Vaccinioideae. It is also classified as a flowering plant, with showy flowers that are an important part of its reproductive cycle.

intro: Leandra riograndensis, also known as "erva-do-bugre," is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. Leandra riograndensis contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
